title: Plant Genetics and Breeding
summary: Linking physiological traits with germplasm diversity to support resilient crop improvement.
description: We evaluate diverse breeding panels and pre-breeding material to determine how physiological traits contribute to adaptation under contrasting environmental conditions. Our work emphasizes trait stability, genotype-by-environment interaction, and phenotypic interpretation that can inform breeding decisions.
relatedPublications: 2025 Quantitative trait stability in durum wheat

title: Stress Physiology
summary: Understanding water deficit, salinity, and heat responses through mechanistic measurements.
description: Our stress physiology projects resolve how leaves, roots, and whole plants adjust carbon gain, osmotic balance, and water status during environmental stress. We design experiments around interpretable stress progression rather than short-term stress exposure alone.
relatedPublications: 2025 Salinity-driven shifts in tomato carbon allocation
2023 Thermal stress recovery across barley accessions

title: Controlled Environment Phenotyping
summary: Measuring growth, architecture, and stress progression in reproducible greenhouse and chamber systems.
description: We use controlled-environment facilities to test hypotheses under tightly managed photoperiod, temperature, irrigation, and nutrition regimes. These experiments support calibration studies, method development, and validation of field observations.
relatedPublications: 2022 Practical standards for pot experiment reproducibility

title: Analytical Chemistry and HPLC Workflows
summary: Quantifying pigments, organic acids, and stress-associated metabolites with transparent laboratory workflows.
description: Analytical chemistry in the lab is focused on robust extraction, calibration, and reporting practices. We routinely maintain SOPs for sample handling, chromatographic separation, blank correction, and reference standards so that metabolite measurements remain comparable across student cohorts and projects.
relatedPublications: 2024 Practical QA for plant metabolite quantification
2021 Pigment extraction sensitivity across tissue types

title: Experimental Design and Statistical Analysis
summary: Building experiments around sound randomization, blocking, and reproducible analysis pipelines.
description: A substantial portion of our group effort is devoted to experimental planning before data collection begins. We help collaborators define treatment structure, blocking strategy, sample size logic, and analysis plans that align with the biological question and realistic sources of variation.
